  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.

  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required.   aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.

  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. # Available Actions
actions[58]:
- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owne
